Safety Reminders

Meet Public

Always meet in public places, especially for a first meetup.

Share Details

Trust Instincts

Guard Your Info

Guides

Verified local drivers & guides for your trip

Request Custom

Tap a category to browse verified local guides for your Kerala trip, or request a custom guide for something specific.

Guide Safety Tips

Verify First

Confirm ID, the verified badge, and agree on the exact price and itinerary before starting any trip.

Share Your Location

Share live location with a friend or family member for the duration of the trip, especially for remote treks.

Avoid Isolated Routes

Avoid isolated trails or backwaters after dark unless your guide is well-reviewed and the route is well known.

Wayanad Wayanad

Vagamon Idukki
Athirappilly Thrissur
Ponmudi Thiruvananthapuram

Camping Safety Tips

Check Permits First

Many forest and hill camping spots need prior Forest Department or local authority permission — confirm before you go.

Stay With a Guide in Wildlife Zones

Never camp or trek unaccompanied near forest and wildlife sanctuary trails — go with a verified local guide.

Douse Campfires Fully

Keep bonfires small and contained, and make sure they're completely out with water before you leave or sleep.

Share Your Location

Share your live location and campsite name with someone back home, especially for remote or multi-day camps.

Pack for Sudden Weather

Kerala's hills and forests can turn cold or wet fast — carry rain cover, warm layers, and a torch.

ePass

Entry permits required for Kerala's parks & reserves

Several forests, wildlife sanctuaries and national parks in Kerala allow only a limited number of visitors a day and require an online entry pass booked in advance. Tap a place below to open its official booking site.

Periyar Tiger Reserve Thekkady, Idukki

Boating, trekking & bamboo rafting need a forest entry pass.

Visit Site
Eravikulam National Park Munnar, Idukki

Daily visitor quota for Rajamala; online entry ticket needed.

Visit Site
Parambikulam Tiger Reserve Palakkad

Safari, trekking & stay all need a reserve entry permit.

Visit Site
Gavi Eco-Tourism Pathanamthitta

Day-visit & jeep safari passes for limited daily slots.

Visit Site
Silent Valley National Park Mannarkkad, Palakkad

Trekking & safari permits limited to protect the rainforest core.

Visit Site
Wayanad & Other Sanctuaries Statewide

Safari & eco-tourism passes for Kerala's other forest divisions.

Visit Site

How ePass Booking Works

1. Pick a Date & Slot

Choose your visit date and a time slot on the official site — most reserves cap the number of visitors per day.

2. Enter Visitor Details

Add names and a valid government ID for each visitor — you'll need to carry the original ID on the day.

3. Pay Online

Complete payment on the official site and save the e-ticket or confirmation SMS/email you receive.

4. Carry Your Pass

Show the printed or mobile e-pass along with original ID at the entry counter to be let in.

Kerala Forest Ecotourism Portal Browse and book passes for every forest division in one place

Kochi Metro

Stations, map, timings & fares

All 25 stations of the Aluva – Tripunithura corridor, plotted in order. Tap a marker for the station name and its position on the line.

Headway (gap between trains) per KMRL's official timetable, effective 17 Feb 2026. Schedules can change — check the official KMRL timetable before you travel.

Estimate your fare

Pick two stations Single journey · KMRL
—
Book on Metro App KMRL Website
Skip the queue with a rechargeable Kochi1 Card (also works on Kochi's buses & boats), or buy a QR ticket straight from the app — no counter visit needed.

Fares shown are an approximate estimate based on the number of stations travelled — always confirm the exact fare in the app or at the station before booking.

Compass

Find your bearings on the go

Mobile or Tablet Required

The compass uses your device's built-in motion sensors, which desktop and laptop computers don't have. Please open Yathra Map on a mobile phone or tablet to use this tool.

Enable the Compass

This tool uses your device's motion sensors to show which way you're facing.

--°
Point your phone to begin

For best accuracy, hold your phone flat and move it in a slow figure-8 motion to calibrate the sensor.

Emergency Assistance

Ambulance Police Fire Women Helpline Child Helpline Disaster Mgmt Tourist Police Tourism Helpline Health Helpline Blood Donors Highway Alert

Safety Tips

Natural Safety

Swim only in lifeguard zones. Beware of strong currents in rivers and backwaters. Avoid trekking during heavy rain. Stay away from cliff edges. Carry a flashlight during forest or night trips.

Health Tips

Drink bottled or purified water. Carry ORS and essential medicines. Use mosquito repellent in monsoon. Wear light cotton clothing. Ensure travel insurance coverage.

Travel Safety

Use ride apps or prepaid taxis. Always wear helmets and seatbelts. Avoid isolated places at night. Check weather alerts during monsoon. Follow beach and backwater safety rules.

Why Hire Through Yathra Map

Verified ID & license
Background checked
Rated by real travelers
Direct contact, no middleman

Hiring a Driver — Safety Tips

Check License First

Ask to see the driving license and match the photo before starting any trip, even for a short transfer.

Agree on the Rate

Confirm the per-day or per-km rate, toll/parking terms, and overnight halt charges before you set off.

Share the Trip Plan

Share the driver's name, phone number and vehicle number with family or friends for the full trip.

Prefer Verified Drivers

Look for the verified badge and a healthy trip count — both are a quick signal of a reliable driver.
